relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Famine returned, and Isaac entered the old Abraham pattern in Gerar.
The Eternal appeared and told him not to go down into Egypt, but to remain where he was shown.
Isaac was called to sojourn in promise, with blessing, presence, land, and seed renewed.
The star-seed promise and blessing for all nations passed forward through Isaac.
Abraham’s listening became part of the covenant memory carried into Isaac’s life.
Isaac stayed in Gerar.
Fear repeated itself: Rebekah was called sister instead of wife.
The truth showed through intimacy, and Abimelech saw Isaac with Rebekah.
The deception was named, and Isaac admitted fear for his life.
Abimelech saw the danger Isaac had brought upon the people.
A boundary was set around Isaac and Rebekah for protection.
Isaac sowed in famine-land and received a hundredfold, because blessing was with him.
He became great, then greater, until his abundance could not be ignored.
The Philistines envied the weight of his blessing.
They stopped up the wells of Abraham, closing old sources of life.
Abimelech sent Isaac away because his strength had become too great.
Isaac moved into the valley and dwelled there.
He re-dug the wells of his father and restored their names.
His servants found living water in the valley.
The first well became conflict, named Esek.
The second well became opposition, named Sitnah.
The third well opened spaciousness, Rehoboth, where fruitfulness could breathe.
Isaac went up to Beersheba.
The Eternal appeared in the night with the promise: Do not fear, I am with you.
Isaac built an altar, called on the name, pitched his tent, and dug a well.
Abimelech came with his men to seek covenant.
Isaac named the wound: you hated me and sent me away.
They admitted what they had seen: the Eternal was with Isaac.
They asked for peace with the one now clearly blessed.
Isaac made a feast, turning conflict toward table fellowship.
An oath was sworn, and they departed in peace.
That same day, water was found.
The well was named Shibah, and Beersheba’s oath-memory continued.
Esau took Hittite women, stepping outside the family’s guarded covenant path.
Those marriages became bitterness of spirit to Isaac and Rebekah.
